subject: Geography
title: Sinkholes
description: A fast breakdown of Sinkholes for builders.

hook.text: In 2010, a sinkhole 60 meters deep swallowed a three-story building in Guatemala City overnight.

buildup.text: Sinkholes form when underground caves collapse—the ground simply gives way.

discovery.text: Florida sits on limestone riddled with cavities. Hundreds of sinkholes open there every year.

twist.text: Pumping groundwater accelerates collapse—removing the water that held the rock in place.

climax.text: Beneath stable-looking ground, hidden caves can be quietly growing for centuries.

punchline.text: The ground beneath your feet might not be as solid as you think.

quiz.question: What causes sinkholes?
options: Tidal forces pulling the earth apart
